Famous blue screens remind conference of security pros that this OS sometimes has bad days

articles.title: AFC Ajax drops ball as flaws let hackers play admin with tickets and bans
articles.summary:
Vulns in Dutch football club's systems didn't just expose data – they let outsiders play with accounts, and even lift stadium bans

Dutch football giant AFC Ajax has admitted to a data breach after an attacker gained access to its internal systems, in an incident that looks less like a stray pass and more like the gates left wide open.…
articles.reasoning: The article describes a data breach involving exposed APIs and digital keys, which relates to how data is stored, accessed, and retrieved in Ajax's systems, though the focus is more on security flaws than the technologies themselves.

articles.title: Lloyds bank reveals IT glitch affected almost half a million customers

articles.title: Lloyds app glitch turned transactions into shared experience for 447k users
articles.summary:
A botched update mixed up transaction data across accounts, with thousands now receiving goodwill payouts

A botched overnight software update at Lloyds Banking Group left up to 447,000 customers briefly seeing other people's transactions in its mobile apps, with the bank now acknowledging the scale of the incident and compensating affected users.…
articles.reasoning: The article describes a major IT service disruption caused by a software update, detailing the incident's cause, impact on users, timeline, and the bank's response including compensation, which aligns directly with incident management processes.

articles.title: ICE Agents Frustrate Airport Workers as Shutdown Drags On
articles.summary: “ICE are here and they’re doing literally nothing to help,” said an airline worker in New York.
articles.reasoning: The article describes a disruption (airport security delays) caused by a government shutdown, leading to TSA staff shortages and the deployment of ICE agents as an unplanned response. This resembles an incident where normal service operations are impaired, requiring management and resolution, though the context is governmental rather than typical IT service management.
